macos_preflight.notes: Mac Ethernet was manually set to the recovery subnet. POC App bound UDP to 192.168.1.10 and sent from a local UDP socket.

recovery_entry.led_state_observed: Power LED slow flashing; LAN1 white LED active during recovery-like state.

readiness_observations.notes: Earlier passive capture observed no router-initiated TFTP RRQ. POC App WRQ was accepted immediately during TTL=100 state.

transfer.first_packet_timing: WRQ sent during observed Rescue Mode / TTL=100 window.

post_upload.notes: After upload completion and more than 5 minutes of waiting, router front state was only LAN1 white LED solid with other LEDs off. Mac wired interface switched to DHCP for about 1 minute and received only a self-assigned 169.254.x.x address with no router/gateway/DNS. After a normal power cycle, DHCP returned on 192.168.50.0/24, Mac received 192.168.50.5 with gateway/DNS 192.168.50.1, and admin UI opened at 192.168.50.1. Stability follow-up confirmed admin UI, firmware page, DHCP page, Wi-Fi settings, WAN connectivity, and existing JFFS ShellCrash installation were still usable.

notes: POC App log still labels the target as R7000 in UI text, but the observed target device for this runtime attempt is ASUS RT-AX86U. POC measured upload duration was 39.956 seconds at 2123.94 KiB/s; schema stores integer duration. WRQ attempt #1 was accepted immediately, so retry behavior was not needed. Configuration_state=retained describes this single RT-AX86U attempt only and is supported by LAN/DHCP/Wi-Fi/WAN/JFFS ShellCrash follow-up checks. Prior RT-AC86U repeated tests reportedly produced mixed retention/reset outcomes, so configuration retention must not be presented as controllable or guaranteed for ASUS Rescue/TFTP workflows.
